names.description: Names of the fields to limit.

resourceAttributes.description: Defines additional resource attributes to be sent to the collector.

additionalArguments.description:
Additional arguments to be passed at Traefik's binary
See [CLI Reference](https://docs.traefik.io/reference/static-configuration/cli/)
Use curly braces to pass values: `helm install --set="additionalArguments={--providers.kubernetesingress.ingressclass=traefik-internal,--log.level=DEBUG}"`
- "--providers.kubernetesingress.ingressclass=traefik-internal"
- "--log.level=DEBUG"

additionalVolumeMounts.description: Additional volumeMounts to add to the Traefik container

affinity.description:
on nodes where no other traefik pods are scheduled.
It should be used when hostNetwork: true to prevent port conflicts
podAntiAffinity:
requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution:
- labelSelector:
matchLabels:
app.kubernetes.io/name: '{{ template "traefik.name" . }}'
app.kubernetes.io/instance: '{{ .Release.Name }}-{{ include "traefik.namespace" . }}'
topologyKey: kubernetes.io/hostname

env.description: Additional Environment variables to be passed to Traefik's binary

envFrom.description: Environment variables to be passed to Traefik's binary from configMaps or secrets

abortOnPluginFailure.description: Defines whether all plugins must be loaded successfully for Traefik to start

hostNetwork.description:
If hostNetwork is true, runs traefik in the host network namespace
To prevent unschedulable pods due to port collisions, if hostNetwork=true
and replicas>1, a pod anti-affinity is recommended and will be set if the
affinity is left as default.

nodeSelector.description: nodeSelector is the simplest recommended form of node selection constraint.

subPath.description: Only mount a subpath of the Volume into the pod

expose.description:
You may not want to expose the metrics port on production deployments.
If you want to access it from outside your cluster,
use `kubectl port-forward` or create a secure ingress

exposedPort.description: The exposed port for this service

port.description:
When using hostNetwork, use another port to avoid conflict with node exporter:
https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/wiki/Default-port-allocations

expose.description:
You SHOULD NOT expose the traefik port on production deployments.
If you want to access it from outside your cluster,
use `kubectl port-forward` or create a secure ingress

exposedPort.description: The exposed port for this service

hostIP.description:
Use hostIP if set. If not set, Kubernetes will default to 0.0.0.0, which
means it's listening on all your interfaces and all your IPs. You may want
to set this value if you need traefik to listen on specific interface
only.

asDefault.description: Enable this entrypoint as a default entrypoint. When a service doesn't explicitly set an entrypoint it will only use this entrypoint.

notAppendXForwardedFor.description: Disable appending RemoteAddr to X-Forwarded-For header (v3.7+).

entryPoint.description:
Port Redirections
Added in 2.2, one can make permanent redirects via entrypoints.
Same sets of parameters: to, scheme, permanent and priority.
https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/reference/install-configuration/entrypoints/#configuration-example

trustedIPs.description: Enable the Proxy Protocol header parsing for the entry point

targetPort.description: Different target traefik port on the cluster, useful for IP type LB

transport.description: Set transport settings for the entrypoint

uplink.description:
Enable this port as an uplink for multi cluster.
⚠️ This feature is experimental and requires Traefik Hub with a specific subscription.

notAppendXForwardedFor.description: Disable appending RemoteAddr to X-Forwarded-For header (v3.7+).

maxHeaderBytes.description: Maximum size of request headers in bytes. Default: 1048576 (1 MB)

enabled.description: See [upstream documentation](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/reference/install-configuration/entrypoints/#opt-http-tls)

underscoreHeadersStrategy.description:
Defines how request headers with underscores in their names are handled (v3.7.6+).
See [upstream documentation](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/reference/install-configuration/entrypoints/#underscoreheadersstrategy)

enabled.description:
Enable HTTP/3 on the entrypoint
Enabling it will also enable http3 experimental feature
https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/reference/install-configuration/entrypoints/#opt-http3
There are known limitations when trying to listen on same ports for
TCP & UDP (Http3). There is a workaround in this chart using dual Service.
https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/47249#issuecomment-587960741

port.description:
Enable this entrypoint as a default entrypoint. When a service doesn't explicitly set an entrypoint it will only use this entrypoint.
asDefault: true

trustedIPs.description: Enable the Proxy Protocol header parsing for the entry point

targetPort.description: Different target traefik port on the cluster, useful for IP type LB

precedence.description: Defines the routing precedence between providers. See [upstream documentation](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/reference/install-configuration/providers/overview/#routing-precedence) for the default order.

serviceAccount.description: The service account the pods will use to interact with the Kubernetes API

tolerations.description: Tolerations allow the scheduler to schedule pods with matching taints.

topologySpreadConstraints.description:
You can use topology spread constraints to control
how Pods are spread across your cluster among failure-domains.
This example topologySpreadConstraints forces the scheduler to put traefik pods
on nodes where no other traefik pods are scheduled.
- labelSelector:
matchLabels:
app.kubernetes.io/name: '{{ template "traefik.name" . }}'
maxSkew: 1
topologyKey: kubernetes.io/hostname
whenUnsatisfiable: DoNotSchedule

additionalArguments.description:
Additional arguments to be passed at Traefik's binary
See [CLI Reference](https://docs.traefik.io/reference/static-configuration/cli/)
Use curly braces to pass values: `helm install --set="additionalArguments={--providers.kubernetesingress.ingressclass=traefik-internal,--log.level=DEBUG}"`
- "--providers.kubernetesingress.ingressclass=traefik-internal"
- "--log.level=DEBUG"

on nodes where no other traefik pods are scheduled.

healthchecksHost.description:
Override the liveness/readiness host. Useful for getting ping to respond on non-default entryPoint.
Default: ports.traefik.hostIP if set, otherwise Pod IP

replicas.description:
Number of pods of the deployment (only applies when kind == Deployment).
Set to null to omit spec.replicas, e.g. when an external controller (HPA/KEDA) owns scaling.

name.description: Set a custom name to gateway

namespace.description: By default, Gateway is created in the same `Namespace` as Traefik.

azure.description:
Required for Azure Marketplace integration.
See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/partner-center/marketplace-offers/azure-container-technical-assets-kubernetes?tabs=linux,linux2#update-the-helm-chart

Required for Azure Marketplace integration.
See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/partner-center/marketplace-offers/azure-container-technical-assets-kubernetes?tabs=linux,linux2#update-the-helm-chart

filePath.description: To write the logs into a log file, use the filePath option.

level.description: Alternative logging levels are TRACE, DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR, FATAL, and PANIC.

resourceAttributes.description: Defines additional resource attributes to be sent to the collector.

metrics.description: metrics contains the specifications for which to use to calculate the desired replica count (the maximum replica count across all metrics will be used).

otlp.description:
datadog:
Address instructs exporter to send metrics to datadog-agent at this address.
address: "127.0.0.1:8125"
The interval used by the exporter to push metrics to datadog-agent. Default=10s
pushInterval: 30s
The prefix to use for metrics collection. Default="traefik"
prefix: traefik
Enable metrics on entry points. Default=true
addEntryPointsLabels: false
Enable metrics on routers. Default=false
addRoutersLabels: true
Enable metrics on services. Default=true
addServicesLabels: false
influxdb2:
Address instructs exporter to send metrics to influxdb v2 at this address.
address: localhost:8086
Token with which to connect to InfluxDB v2.
token: xxx
Organisation where metrics will be stored.
org: ""
Bucket where metrics will be stored.
bucket: ""
The interval used by the exporter to push metrics to influxdb. Default=10s
pushInterval: 30s
Additional labels (influxdb tags) on all metrics.
additionalLabels:
env: production
foo: bar
Enable metrics on entry points. Default=true
addEntryPointsLabels: false
Enable metrics on routers. Default=false
addRoutersLabels: true
Enable metrics on services. Default=true
addServicesLabels: false
statsd:
Address instructs exporter to send metrics to statsd at this address.
address: localhost:8125
The interval used by the exporter to push metrics to influxdb. Default=10s
pushInterval: 30s
The prefix to use for metrics collection. Default="traefik"
prefix: traefik
Enable metrics on entry points. Default=true
addEntryPointsLabels: false
Enable metrics on routers. Default=false
addRoutersLabels: true
Enable metrics on services. Default=true
addServicesLabels: false

explicitBoundaries.description: Explicit boundaries for Histogram data points. Default: [.005, .01, .025, .05, .1, .25, .5, 1, 2.5, 5, 10]

pushInterval.description: Interval at which metrics are sent to the OpenTelemetry Collector. Default: 10s

resourceAttributes.description: Defines additional resource attributes to be sent to the collector.

metrics.description: metrics contains the specifications for which to use to calculate the desired replica count (the maximum replica count across all metrics will be used).

prometheus.description:
Prometheus is enabled by default.
It can be disabled by setting "prometheus: null"

entryPoint.description: Entry point used to expose metrics.

headerLabels.description: Add HTTP header labels to metrics. See EXAMPLES.md or upstream doc for usage.

oci_meta.description:
Required for OCI Marketplace integration.
See https://docs.public.content.oci.oraclecloud.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Marketplace/understanding-helm-charts.htm

Required for OCI Marketplace integration.
See https://docs.public.content.oci.oraclecloud.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Marketplace/understanding-helm-charts.htm

Required for OCI Marketplace integration.
See https://docs.public.content.oci.oraclecloud.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Marketplace/understanding-helm-charts.htm

repo.description: It needs to be an ocir repo

port.description:
When using hostNetwork, use another port to avoid conflict with node exporter:
https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/wiki/Default-port-allocations

crossProviderNamespaces.description: List of namespaces from which IngressRoute, IngressRouteTCP, IngressRouteUDP, and TraefikService are allowed to declare cross-provider references. Requires traefik v3.7.1+.
List of namespaces from which Gateway API routes are allowed to declare TraefikService backendRef references. Requires traefik v3.7.1+.

labelSelector.description: A label selector can be defined to filter on specific GatewayClass objects only.

hostname.description: This Hostname will get copied to the Gateway status.addresses.

ip.description: This IP will get copied to the Gateway status.addresses, and currently only supports one IP value (IPv4 or IPv6).

crossProviderNamespaces.description: List of namespaces from which Ingresses or Services are allowed to declare Middlewares, TLSOptions, or ServersTransport references. Requires traefik v3.7.1+.

hostname.description: Hostname used for Kubernetes Ingress endpoints

ip.description: IP used for Kubernetes Ingress endpoints

serviceAccount.description: The service account the pods will use to interact with the Kubernetes API

serviceAccountAnnotations.description: Additional serviceAccount annotations (e.g. for oidc authentication)

addInternals.description: Enables accessLogs for internal resources. Default: false.

dualOutput.description: Enables access log output alongside OTLP (v3.7+).

enabled.description: To enable access logs

names.description: Names of the fields to limit.

defaultMode.description: Keep or drop all query parameters in the RequestPath access log field (v3.7.3+).

enabled.description: Set to true in order to enable OpenTelemetry on access logs. Note that experimental.otlpLogs needs to be enabled.
Set to true in order to send access logs to the OpenTelemetry Collector using gRPC

insecure.description: Allows reporter to send access logs to the OpenTelemetry Collector without using a secured protocol.

tls.description: Defines the TLS configuration used by the reporter to send access logs to the OpenTelemetry Collector.

ca.description: The path to the certificate authority, it defaults to the system bundle.

cert.description: The path to the public certificate. When using this option, setting the key option is required.

insecureSkipVerify.description: When set to true, the TLS connection accepts any certificate presented by the server regardless of the hostnames it covers.

key.description: The path to the private key. When using this option, setting the cert option is required.

enabled.description: Set to true in order to send access logs to the OpenTelemetry Collector using HTTP.

headers.description: Additional headers sent with access logs by the reporter to the OpenTelemetry Collector.

tls.description: Defines the TLS configuration used by the reporter to send access logs to the OpenTelemetry Collector.

ca.description: The path to the certificate authority, it defaults to the system bundle.

cert.description: The path to the public certificate. When using this option, setting the key option is required.

insecureSkipVerify.description: When set to true, the TLS connection accepts any certificate presented by the server regardless of the hostnames it covers.

key.description: The path to the private key. When using this option, setting the cert option is required.

resourceAttributes.description: Defines additional resource attributes to be sent to the collector.

serviceName.description: Service name used in OTLP backend. Default: traefik.

additionalArguments.description:
Additional arguments to be passed at Traefik's binary
See [CLI Reference](https://docs.traefik.io/reference/static-configuration/cli/)
Use curly braces to pass values: `helm install --set="additionalArguments={--providers.kubernetesingress.ingressclass=traefik-internal,--log.level=DEBUG}"`
- "--providers.kubernetesingress.ingressclass=traefik-internal"
- "--log.level=DEBUG"

certificatesResolvers.description:
Certificates resolvers configuration.
Ref: https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/reference/install-configuration/tls/certificate-resolvers/acme/
See EXAMPLES.md for more details.

commonLabels.description: Add additional label to all resources

additionalContainers.description:
Additional containers (e.g. for metric offloading sidecars)
https://docs.datadoghq.com/developers/dogstatsd/unix_socket/?tab=host
- name: socat-proxy
image: alpine/socat:1.0.5
args: ["-s", "-u", "udp-recv:8125", "unix-sendto:/socket/socket"]
volumeMounts:
- name: dsdsocket
mountPath: /socket

additionalVolumes.description:
Additional volumes available for use with initContainers and additionalContainers
- name: dsdsocket
hostPath:
path: /var/run/statsd-exporter

annotations.description: Additional deployment annotations (e.g. for jaeger-operator sidecar injection)

dnsPolicy.description: Custom pod DNS policy. Apply if `hostNetwork: true`

healthchecksScheme.description:
Override the liveness/readiness scheme. Useful for getting ping to
respond on websecure entryPoint.

imagePullSecrets.description: Pull secret for fetching traefik container image

initContainers.description:
Additional initContainers (e.g. for setting file permission as shown below)
The "volume-permissions" init container is required if you run into permission issues.
Related issue: https://github.com/traefik/traefik-helm-chart/issues/396
- name: volume-permissions
image: busybox:latest
command: ["sh", "-c", "touch /data/acme.json; chmod -v 600 /data/acme.json"]
volumeMounts:
- name: data
mountPath: /data

labels.description: Additional deployment labels (e.g. for filtering deployment by custom labels)

lifecycle.description:
Pod lifecycle actions
preStop:
sleep:
seconds: 20
postStart:
httpGet:
path: /ping
port: 8080
host: localhost
scheme: HTTP

minReadySeconds.description: The minimum number of seconds Traefik needs to be up and running before the DaemonSet/Deployment controller considers it available

podAnnotations.description:
Additional pod annotations (e.g. for mesh injection or prometheus scraping)
It supports templating. One can set it with values like traefik/name: '{{ template "traefik.name" . }}'

podLabels.description:
Additional Pod labels (e.g. for filtering Pod by custom labels)
It supports templating. One can set it with values like traefik/name: '{{ template "traefik.name" . }}'

terminationGracePeriodSeconds.description: Amount of time (in seconds) before Kubernetes will send the SIGKILL signal if Traefik does not shut down

additionalContainers.description:
Additional containers (e.g. for metric offloading sidecars)
https://docs.datadoghq.com/developers/dogstatsd/unix_socket/?tab=host
- name: socat-proxy
image: alpine/socat:1.0.5
args: ["-s", "-u", "udp-recv:8125", "unix-sendto:/socket/socket"]
volumeMounts:
- name: dsdsocket
mountPath: /socket

Additional volumes available for use with initContainers and additionalContainers
- name: dsdsocket
hostPath:
path: /var/run/statsd-exporter

annotations.description: Additional deployment annotations (e.g. for jaeger-operator sidecar injection)

dnsPolicy.description: Custom pod DNS policy. Apply if `hostNetwork: true`

enabled.description: Enable deployment

goMemLimitPercentage.description: Percentage of memory limit to set for GOMEMLIMIT, set as decimal (0.9 = 90%, 0.95 = 95% etc). Only takes effect when resources.limits.memory is set. Set to 0 to disable (e.g. when using VPA or setting it via env)

healthchecksHost.description:
Override the liveness/readiness host. Useful for getting ping to respond on non-default entryPoint.
Default: ports.traefik.hostIP if set, otherwise Pod IP

healthchecksPort.description:
Override the liveness/readiness port. This is useful to integrate traefik
with an external Load Balancer that performs healthchecks.
Default: ports.traefik.port

healthchecksScheme.description:
Override the liveness/readiness scheme. Useful for getting ping to
respond on websecure entryPoint.

hostUsers.description:
Whether to use the host user namespace. Setting this to false enables user namespaces,
which can improve security by isolating the pod's users from the host.
See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/user-namespaces/

imagePullSecrets.description: Pull secret for fetching traefik container image

initContainers.description:
Additional initContainers (e.g. for setting file permission as shown below)
The "volume-permissions" init container is required if you run into permission issues.
Related issue: https://github.com/traefik/traefik-helm-chart/issues/396
- name: volume-permissions
image: busybox:latest
command: ["sh", "-c", "touch /data/acme.json; chmod -v 600 /data/acme.json"]
volumeMounts:
- name: data
mountPath: /data

labels.description: Additional deployment labels (e.g. for filtering deployment by custom labels)

lifecycle.description:
Pod lifecycle actions
preStop:
sleep:
seconds: 20
postStart:
httpGet:
path: /ping
port: 8080
host: localhost
scheme: HTTP

minReadySeconds.description: The minimum number of seconds Traefik needs to be up and running before the DaemonSet/Deployment controller considers it available

podAnnotations.description:
Additional pod annotations (e.g. for mesh injection or prometheus scraping)
It supports templating. One can set it with values like traefik/name: '{{ template "traefik.name" . }}'

podLabels.description:
Additional Pod labels (e.g. for filtering Pod by custom labels)
It supports templating. One can set it with values like traefik/name: '{{ template "traefik.name" . }}'

replicas.description:
Number of pods of the deployment (only applies when kind == Deployment).
Set to null to omit spec.replicas, e.g. when an external controller (HPA/KEDA) owns scaling.

revisionHistoryLimit.description: Number of old history to retain to allow rollback (If not set, default Kubernetes value is set to 10)

terminationGracePeriodSeconds.description: Amount of time (in seconds) before Kubernetes will send the SIGKILL signal if Traefik does not shut down

enabled.description:
Create a default IngressClass for Traefik
When providers.kubernetesGateway.enabled and gateway.enabled, deploy a default gatewayClass
Create HorizontalPodAutoscaler object.
See EXAMPLES.md for more details.
Whether Role Based Access Control objects like roles and rolebindings should be created
Allow the Helm chart to be used as optional subchart.

env.description: Additional Environment variables to be passed to Traefik's binary

envFrom.description: Environment variables to be passed to Traefik's binary from configMaps or secrets

abortOnPluginFailure.description: Defines whether all plugins must be loaded successfully for Traefik to start

debug.description: Enable debug mode for the FastProxy implementation.

enabled.description: Enables the FastProxy implementation.
Enable traefik experimental GatewayClass CRD

extraObjects.description:
Extra objects to deploy (value evaluated as a template)
In some cases, it can avoid the need for additional, extended or adhoc deployments.
See #595 for more details and traefik/tests/values/extra.yaml for example.

fullnameOverride.description: Overrides the resource name for templates (i.e deployment, service, etc..)

defaultScope.description:
Configure this Gateway as a [Default Gateway](https://kubernetes.io/blog/2025/11/06/gateway-api-v1-4/#introducing-default-gateways)
by setting the `defaultScope` field (e.g. `All` or `Namespace`).

enabled.description: When providers.kubernetesGateway.enabled, deploy a default gateway

namespacePolicy.description:
(object) Routes are restricted to namespace of the gateway [by default](https://gateway-api.sigs.k8s.io/reference/spec/#gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1.FromNamespaces
websecure listener is disabled by default because certificateRefs needs to be added,
or you may specify TLS protocol with Passthrough mode and add "--providers.kubernetesGateway.experimentalChannel=true" in additionalArguments section.
websecure:

port.description:
Port is the network port. Multiple listeners may use the same port, subject to the Listener compatibility rules.
The port must match a port declared in ports section.

protocol.description: Specify expected protocol on this listener. See [ProtocolType](https://gateway-api.sigs.k8s.io/reference/spec/#gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1.ProtocolType)

name.description: Set a custom name to gateway

namespace.description: By default, Gateway is created in the same `Namespace` as Traefik.

azure.description:
Required for Azure Marketplace integration.
See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/partner-center/marketplace-offers/azure-container-technical-assets-kubernetes?tabs=linux,linux2#update-the-helm-chart

notAppendXForwardedFor.description: Disable appending RemoteAddr to X-Forwarded-For header globally (v3.7+).

sendAnonymousUsage.description:
Please take time to consider whether or not you wish to share anonymous data with us
See https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/contributing/data-collection/

hostNetwork.description:
If hostNetwork is true, runs traefik in the host network namespace
To prevent unschedulable pods due to port collisions, if hostNetwork=true
and replicas>1, a pod anti-affinity is recommended and will be set if the
affinity is left as default.

ingressRoute.description:
Only dashboard & healthcheck IngressRoute are supported.
It's recommended to create workloads CR outside of this Chart.

enabled.description: Create an IngressRoute for the dashboard

entryPoints.description:
Specify the allowed entrypoints to use for the dashboard ingress route, (e.g. traefik, web, websecure).
By default, it's using traefik entrypoint, which is not exposed.
/!\ Do not expose your dashboard without any protection over the internet /!\

labels.description: Additional ingressRoute labels (e.g. for filtering IngressRoute by custom labels)

matchRule.description: The router match rule used for the dashboard ingressRoute

services.description: The internal service used for the dashboard ingressRoute

enabled.description: Create an IngressRoute for the healthcheck probe

entryPoints.description:
Specify the allowed entrypoints to use for the healthcheck ingress route, (e.g. traefik, web, websecure).
By default, it's using traefik entrypoint, which is not exposed.

labels.description: Additional ingressRoute labels (e.g. for filtering IngressRoute by custom labels)

matchRule.description: The router match rule used for the healthcheck ingressRoute

services.description: The internal service used for the healthcheck ingressRoute

instanceLabelOverride.description: This field overrides the default app.kubernetes.io/instance label for all Objects.

filePath.description: To write the logs into a log file, use the filePath option.

format.description:
Set [logs format](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/reference/install-configuration/observability/logs-and-accesslogs/#opt-log-format)
By default, the level is set to INFO.

level.description: Alternative logging levels are TRACE, DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR, FATAL, and PANIC.

noColor.description: When set to true and format is common, it disables the colorized output.

enabled.description: Set to true in order to enable OpenTelemetry on logs. Note that experimental.otlpLogs needs to be enabled.
Set to true in order to send logs  to the OpenTelemetry Collector using gRPC

insecure.description: Allows reporter to send logs to the OpenTelemetry Collector without using a secured protocol.

tls.description: Defines the TLS configuration used by the reporter to send logs to the OpenTelemetry Collector.

ca.description: The path to the certificate authority, it defaults to the system bundle.

cert.description: The path to the public certificate. When using this option, setting the key option is required.

insecureSkipVerify.description: When set to true, the TLS connection accepts any certificate presented by the server regardless of the hostnames it covers.

key.description: The path to the private key. When using this option, setting the cert option is required.

enabled.description: Set to true in order to send logs to the OpenTelemetry Collector using HTTP.

headers.description: Additional headers sent with logs by the reporter to the OpenTelemetry Collector.

tls.description: Defines the TLS configuration used by the reporter to send logs to the OpenTelemetry Collector.

ca.description: The path to the certificate authority, it defaults to the system bundle.

cert.description: The path to the public certificate. When using this option, setting the key option is required.

insecureSkipVerify.description: When set to true, the TLS connection accepts any certificate presented by the server regardless of the hostnames it covers.

key.description: The path to the private key. When using this option, setting the cert option is required.

resourceAttributes.description: Defines additional resource attributes to be sent to the collector.

serviceName.description: Service name used in OTLP backend. Default: traefik.

metrics.description: metrics contains the specifications for which to use to calculate the desired replica count (the maximum replica count across all metrics will be used).

addInternals.description: Enable metrics for internal resources. Default: false

otlp.description:
datadog:
Address instructs exporter to send metrics to datadog-agent at this address.
address: "127.0.0.1:8125"
The interval used by the exporter to push metrics to datadog-agent. Default=10s
pushInterval: 30s
The prefix to use for metrics collection. Default="traefik"
prefix: traefik
Enable metrics on entry points. Default=true
addEntryPointsLabels: false
Enable metrics on routers. Default=false
addRoutersLabels: true
Enable metrics on services. Default=true
addServicesLabels: false
influxdb2:
Address instructs exporter to send metrics to influxdb v2 at this address.
address: localhost:8086
Token with which to connect to InfluxDB v2.
token: xxx
Organisation where metrics will be stored.
org: ""
Bucket where metrics will be stored.
bucket: ""
The interval used by the exporter to push metrics to influxdb. Default=10s
pushInterval: 30s
Additional labels (influxdb tags) on all metrics.
additionalLabels:
env: production
foo: bar
Enable metrics on entry points. Default=true
addEntryPointsLabels: false
Enable metrics on routers. Default=false
addRoutersLabels: true
Enable metrics on services. Default=true
addServicesLabels: false
statsd:
Address instructs exporter to send metrics to statsd at this address.
address: localhost:8125
The interval used by the exporter to push metrics to influxdb. Default=10s
pushInterval: 30s
The prefix to use for metrics collection. Default="traefik"
prefix: traefik
Enable metrics on entry points. Default=true
addEntryPointsLabels: false
Enable metrics on routers. Default=false
addRoutersLabels: true
Enable metrics on services. Default=true
addServicesLabels: false

addEntryPointsLabels.description: Enable metrics on entry points. Default: true

addRoutersLabels.description: Enable metrics on routers. Default: false

addServicesLabels.description: Enable metrics on services. Default: true

enabled.description: Set to true in order to enable the OpenTelemetry metrics

explicitBoundaries.description: Explicit boundaries for Histogram data points. Default: [.005, .01, .025, .05, .1, .25, .5, 1, 2.5, 5, 10]

enabled.description: Set to true in order to send metrics to the OpenTelemetry Collector using gRPC

insecure.description: Allows reporter to send metrics to the OpenTelemetry Collector without using a secured protocol.

tls.description: Defines the TLS configuration used by the reporter to send metrics to the OpenTelemetry Collector.

ca.description: The path to the certificate authority, it defaults to the system bundle.

cert.description: The path to the public certificate. When using this option, setting the key option is required.

insecureSkipVerify.description: When set to true, the TLS connection accepts any certificate presented by the server regardless of the hostnames it covers.

key.description: The path to the private key. When using this option, setting the cert option is required.

enabled.description: Set to true in order to send metrics to the OpenTelemetry Collector using HTTP.

headers.description: Additional headers sent with metrics by the reporter to the OpenTelemetry Collector.

tls.description: Defines the TLS configuration used by the reporter to send metrics to the OpenTelemetry Collector.

ca.description: The path to the certificate authority, it defaults to the system bundle.

cert.description: The path to the public certificate. When using this option, setting the key option is required.

insecureSkipVerify.description: When set to true, the TLS connection accepts any certificate presented by the server regardless of the hostnames it covers.

key.description: The path to the private key. When using this option, setting the cert option is required.

pushInterval.description: Interval at which metrics are sent to the OpenTelemetry Collector. Default: 10s

resourceAttributes.description: Defines additional resource attributes to be sent to the collector.

serviceName.description: Service name used in OTLP backend. Default: traefik.

entryPoint.description: Entry point used to expose metrics.

anyOf.description:
APIVersion defines the versioned schema of this representation of an object.
Servers should convert recognized schemas to the latest internal value, and
may reject unrecognized values.
More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#resources
enableHttp2 can be used to disable HTTP2.

followRedirects defines whether the client should follow HTTP 3xx
redirects.

honorLabels defines when true the metric's labels when they collide
with the target's labels.

honorTimestamps defines whether Prometheus preserves the timestamps
when exposed by the target.

interval at which Prometheus scrapes the metrics from the target.

If empty, Prometheus uses the global scrape interval.

jobLabel selects the label from the associated Kubernetes `Service`
object which will be used as the `job` label for all metrics.

For example if `jobLabel` is set to `foo` and the Kubernetes `Service`
object is labeled with `foo: bar`, then Prometheus adds the `job="bar"`
label to all ingested metrics.

If the value of this field is empty or if the label doesn't exist for
the given Service, the `job` label of the metrics defaults to the name
of the associated Kubernetes `Service`.

Endpoint defines an endpoint serving Prometheus metrics to be scraped by
Prometheus.

authorization.description:
authorization configures the Authorization header credentials used by
the client.

Cannot be set at the same time as `basicAuth`, `bearerTokenSecret` or `oauth2`.

credentials.description: credentials defines a key of a Secret in the namespace that contains the credentials for authentication.

key.description: The key of the secret to select from.  Must be a valid secret key.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined

type.description:
type defines the authentication type. The value is case-insensitive.

"Basic" is not a supported value.

Default: "Bearer"

basicAuth.description:
basicAuth defines the Basic Authentication credentials used by the
client.

Cannot be set at the same time as `authorization`, `bearerTokenSecret` or `oauth2`.

password.description:
password defines a key of a Secret containing the password for
authentication.

key.description: The key of the secret to select from.  Must be a valid secret key.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined

username.description:
username defines a key of a Secret containing the username for
authentication.

key.description: The key of the secret to select from.  Must be a valid secret key.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined

bearerTokenFile.description:
bearerTokenFile defines the file to read bearer token for scraping the target.

Deprecated: use `authorization` instead.

bearerTokenSecret.description:
bearerTokenSecret defines a key of a Secret containing the bearer token
used by the client for authentication. The secret needs to be in the
same namespace as the custom resource and readable by the Prometheus
Operator.

Cannot be set at the same time as `authorization`, `basicAuth` or `oauth2`.

Deprecated: use `authorization` instead.

key.description: The key of the secret to select from.  Must be a valid secret key.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

optional.description: Specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined

enableHttp2.description: enableHttp2 can be used to disable HTTP2.

filterRunning.description:
filterRunning when true, the pods which are not running (e.g. either in Failed or
Succeeded state) are dropped during the target discovery.

If unset, the filtering is enabled.

More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle/#pod-phase

followRedirects.description:
followRedirects defines whether the client should follow HTTP 3xx
redirects.

honorLabels.description:
honorLabels defines when true the metric's labels when they collide
with the target's labels.

honorTimestamps.description:
honorTimestamps defines whether Prometheus preserves the timestamps
when exposed by the target.

interval.description:
interval at which Prometheus scrapes the metrics from the target.

If empty, Prometheus uses the global scrape interval.

metricRelabelings.description:
metricRelabelings defines the relabeling rules to apply to the
samples before ingestion.

items.description:
RelabelConfig allows dynamic rewriting of the label set for targets, alerts,
scraped samples and remote write samples.

More info: https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/configuration/#relabel_config

action.description:
action to perform based on the regex matching.

`Uppercase` and `Lowercase` actions require Prometheus >= v2.36.0.
`DropEqual` and `KeepEqual` actions require Prometheus >= v2.41.0.

Default: "Replace"

modulus.description:
modulus to take of the hash of the source label values.

Only applicable when the action is `HashMod`.

regex.description: regex defines the regular expression against which the extracted value is matched.

replacement.description:
replacement value against which a Replace action is performed if the
regular expression matches.

Regex capture groups are available.

separator.description: separator defines the string between concatenated SourceLabels.

sourceLabels.description:
sourceLabels defines the source labels select values from existing labels. Their content is
concatenated using the configured Separator and matched against the
configured regular expression.

items.description:
LabelName is a valid Prometheus label name.
For Prometheus 3.x, a label name is valid if it contains UTF-8 characters.
For Prometheus 2.x, a label name is only valid if it contains ASCII characters, letters, numbers, as well as underscores.

targetLabel.description:
targetLabel defines the label to which the resulting string is written in a replacement.

It is mandatory for `Replace`, `HashMod`, `Lowercase`, `Uppercase`,
`KeepEqual` and `DropEqual` actions.

Regex capture groups are available.

noProxy.description:
noProxy defines a comma-separated string that can contain IPs, CIDR notation, domain names
that should be excluded from proxying. IP and domain names can
contain port numbers.

It requires Prometheus >= v2.43.0, Alertmanager >= v0.25.0 or Thanos >= v0.32.0.

oauth2.description:
oauth2 defines the OAuth2 settings used by the client.

It requires Prometheus >= 2.27.0.

Cannot be set at the same time as `authorization`, `basicAuth` or `bearerTokenSecret`.

clientId.description:
clientId defines a key of a Secret or ConfigMap containing the
OAuth2 client's ID.

configMap.description: configMap defines the ConfigMap containing data to use for the targets.

key.description: The key to select.

allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.

endpointParams.description:

URL.

proxyConnectHeader optionally specifies headers to send to
proxies during CONNECT requests.

It requires Prometheus >= v2.43.0, Alertmanager >= v0.25.0 or Thanos >= v0.32.0.

proxyFromEnvironment.description:
proxyFromEnvironment defines whether to use the proxy configuration defined by environment variables (HTTP_PROXY, HTTPS_PROXY, and NO_PROXY).

It requires Prometheus >= v2.43.0, Alertmanager >= v0.25.0 or Thanos >= v0.32.0.

scopes.description: scopes defines the OAuth2 scopes used for the token request.

tlsConfig.description:

maxVersion defines the maximum acceptable TLS version.

minVersion.description:
minVersion defines the minimum acceptable TLS version.

It requires Prometheus >= v2.35.0 or Thanos >= v0.28.0.

serverName.description: serverName is used to verify the hostname for the targets.

tokenUrl.description: tokenUrl defines the URL to fetch the token from.

params.description: params define optional HTTP URL parameters.

path.description:
path defines the HTTP path from which to scrape for metrics.

If empty, Prometheus uses the default value (e.g. `/metrics`).

port.description:
port defines the name of the Service port which this endpoint refers to.

It takes precedence over `targetPort`.

It requires Prometheus >= v2.43.0, Alertmanager >= v0.25.0 or Thanos >= v0.32.0.

relabelings.description:
relabelings defines the relabeling rules to apply the target's
metadata labels.

The Operator automatically adds relabelings for a few standard Kubernetes fields.

The original scrape job's name is available via the `__tmp_prometheus_job_name` label.

More info: https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/configuration/#relabel_config

items.description:
RelabelConfig allows dynamic rewriting of the label set for targets, alerts,
scraped samples and remote write samples.

More info: https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/configuration/#relabel_config

action.description:
action to perform based on the regex matching.

`Uppercase` and `Lowercase` actions require Prometheus >= v2.36.0.
`DropEqual` and `KeepEqual` actions require Prometheus >= v2.41.0.

Default: "Replace"

modulus.description:
modulus to take of the hash of the source label values.

Only applicable when the action is `HashMod`.

regex.description: regex defines the regular expression against which the extracted value is matched.

replacement.description:
replacement value against which a Replace action is performed if the
regular expression matches.

Regex capture groups are available.

sourceLabels.description:

targetLabel.description:

It is mandatory for `Replace`, `HashMod`, `Lowercase`, `Uppercase`,
`KeepEqual` and `DropEqual` actions.

Regex capture groups are available.

scheme.description: scheme defines the HTTP scheme to use when scraping the metrics.

scrapeTimeout.description:
scrapeTimeout defines the timeout after which Prometheus considers the scrape to be failed.

If empty, Prometheus uses the global scrape timeout unless it is less
than the target's scrape interval value in which the latter is used.
The value cannot be greater than the scrape interval otherwise the operator will reject the resource.

targetPort.description:
targetPort defines the name or number of the target port of the `Pod` object behind the
Service. The port must be specified with the container's port property.

tlsConfig.description: tlsConfig defines TLS configuration used by the client.

ca.description: ca defines the Certificate authority used when verifying server certificates.

maxVersion defines the maximum acceptable TLS version.

It requires Prometheus >= v2.41.0 or Thanos >= v0.31.0.

minVersion.description:
minVersion defines the minimum acceptable TLS version.

It requires Prometheus >= v2.35.0 or Thanos >= v0.28.0.

serverName.description: serverName is used to verify the hostname for the targets.

trackTimestampsStaleness.description:
trackTimestampsStaleness defines whether Prometheus tracks staleness of
the metrics that have an explicit timestamp present in scraped data.
Has no effect if `honorTimestamps` is false.

It requires Prometheus >= v2.48.0.

namespaceSelector.description:
namespaceSelector defines in which namespace(s) Prometheus should discover the services.
By default, the services are discovered in the same namespace as the `ServiceMonitor` object but it is possible to select pods across different/all namespaces.

any.description:
any defines the boolean describing whether all namespaces are selected in contrast to a
list restricting them.

matchNames.description: matchNames defines the list of namespace names to select from.

relabelings.description:
relabelings defines the relabeling rules to apply the target's
metadata labels.

The Operator automatically adds relabelings for a few standard Kubernetes fields.

The original scrape job's name is available via the `__tmp_prometheus_job_name` label.

More info: https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/configuration/#relabel_config

scraped samples and remote write samples.

scrapeTimeout defines the timeout after which Prometheus considers the scrape to be failed.

APIVersion defines the versioned schema of this representation of an object.

rules.description: List of alerting and recording rules.

items.description:
Rule describes an alerting or recording rule
See Prometheus documentation: [alerting](https://www.prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/alerting_rules/) or [recording](https://www.prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/recording_rules/#recording-rules) rule

alert.description:
Name of the alert. Must be a valid label value.
Only one of `record` and `alert` must be set.

annotations.description:
Annotations to add to each alert.
Only valid for alerting rules.

for.description: Alerts are considered firing once they have been returned for this long.

keep_firing_for.description: KeepFiringFor defines how long an alert will continue firing after the condition that triggered it has cleared.

record.description:
Name of the time series to output to. Must be a valid metric name.
Only one of `record` and `alert` must be set.

prometheus.description:
Prometheus is enabled by default.
It can be disabled by setting "prometheus: null"

addEntryPointsLabels.description: Enable metrics on entry points. Default: true

addRoutersLabels.description: Enable metrics on routers. Default: false

addServicesLabels.description: Enable metrics on services. Default: true

disableAPICheck.description: When set to true, it won't check if Prometheus Operator CRDs are deployed

entryPoint.description: Entry point used to expose metrics.

headerLabels.description: Add HTTP header labels to metrics. See EXAMPLES.md or upstream doc for usage.

manualRouting.description:
When manualRouting is true, it disables the default internal router in
order to allow creating a custom router for prometheus@internal service.

enabled.description: Enable optional CR for Prometheus Operator. See EXAMPLES.md for more details.

rules.description: List of alerting and recording rules.

items.description:
Rule describes an alerting or recording rule
See Prometheus documentation: [alerting](https://www.prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/alerting_rules/) or [recording](https://www.prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/recording_rules/#recording-rules) rule

alert.description:
Name of the alert. Must be a valid label value.
Only one of `record` and `alert` must be set.

annotations.description:
Annotations to add to each alert.
Only valid for alerting rules.

for.description: Alerts are considered firing once they have been returned for this long.

keep_firing_for.description: KeepFiringFor defines how long an alert will continue firing after the condition that triggered it has cleared.

record.description:
Name of the time series to output to. Must be a valid metric name.
Only one of `record` and `alert` must be set.

enabled.description: Create a dedicated metrics service to use with ServiceMonitor
Enable optional CR for Prometheus Operator. See EXAMPLES.md for more details.

namespaceSelector.description:
namespaceSelector defines in which namespace(s) Prometheus should discover the services.
By default, the services are discovered in the same namespace as the `ServiceMonitor` object but it is possible to select pods across different/all namespaces.

any.description:
any defines the boolean describing whether all namespaces are selected in contrast to a
list restricting them.

matchNames.description: matchNames defines the list of namespace names to select from.

relabelings.description:
relabelings defines the relabeling rules to apply the target's
metadata labels.

The Operator automatically adds relabelings for a few standard Kubernetes fields.

The original scrape job's name is available via the `__tmp_prometheus_job_name` label.

More info: https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/configuration/#relabel_config

items.description:
RelabelConfig allows dynamic rewriting of the label set for targets, alerts,
scraped samples and remote write samples.

More info: https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/configuration/#relabel_config

action.description:
action to perform based on the regex matching.

`Uppercase` and `Lowercase` actions require Prometheus >= v2.36.0.
`DropEqual` and `KeepEqual` actions require Prometheus >= v2.41.0.

Default: "Replace"

modulus.description:
modulus to take of the hash of the source label values.

Only applicable when the action is `HashMod`.

regex.description: regex defines the regular expression against which the extracted value is matched.

replacement.description:
replacement value against which a Replace action is performed if the
regular expression matches.

Regex capture groups are available.

separator.description: separator defines the string between concatenated SourceLabels.

sourceLabels.description:
sourceLabels defines the source labels select values from existing labels. Their content is
concatenated using the configured Separator and matched against the
configured regular expression.

items.description:
LabelName is a valid Prometheus label name.
For Prometheus 3.x, a label name is valid if it contains UTF-8 characters.
For Prometheus 2.x, a label name is only valid if it contains ASCII characters, letters, numbers, as well as underscores.

targetLabel.description:
targetLabel defines the label to which the resulting string is written in a replacement.

It is mandatory for `Replace`, `HashMod`, `Lowercase`, `Uppercase`,
`KeepEqual` and `DropEqual` actions.

Regex capture groups are available.

namespaceOverride.description:
This field overrides the default Release Namespace for Helm.
It will not affect optional CRDs such as `ServiceMonitor` and `PrometheusRules`

nodeSelector.description: nodeSelector is the simplest recommended form of node selection constraint.

oci_meta.description:
Required for OCI Marketplace integration.
See https://docs.public.content.oci.oraclecloud.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Marketplace/understanding-helm-charts.htm

enabled.description: Enable specific values for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure

repo.description: It needs to be an ocir repo

enabled.description:
Enable OCSP stapling support.
See https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/reference/install-configuration/tls/ocsp/

responderOverrides.description: Defines the OCSP responder URLs to use instead of the one provided by the certificate.

offering_version.description:
Required for IBM Cloud Marketplace integration.
Injected by IBM Cloud Catalog when deploying via IBM Cloud Schematics. This value is not used by the chart.

enabled.description:
Enable persistence using Persistent Volume Claims
ref: http://kubernetes.io/docs/user-guide/persistent-volumes/.
It can be used to store TLS certificates along with `certificatesResolvers.<name>.acme.storage`  option

subPath.description: Only mount a subpath of the Volume into the pod

content.description: File content as an object (will be YAML-formatted, go template supported) (see https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/reference/install-configuration/providers/others/file/)
File content as an object (will be YAML-formatted, go template supported) (see https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/reference/install-configuration/providers/others/file/)

enabled.description: Create a file provider

watch.description: Allows Traefik to automatically watch for file changes

enabled.description: Enable Knative provider

namespaces.description: Array of namespaces to watch. If left empty, Traefik watches all namespaces. . When using `rbac.namespaced`, it will watch helm release namespace and namespaces listed in this array.

allowCrossNamespace.description: Allows IngressRoute to reference resources in namespace other than theirs

allowEmptyServices.description: Allows to return 503 when there are no endpoints available

allowExternalNameServices.description: Allows to reference ExternalName services in IngressRoute

crossProviderNamespaces.description: List of namespaces from which IngressRoute, IngressRouteTCP, IngressRouteUDP, and TraefikService are allowed to declare cross-provider references. Requires traefik v3.7.1+.

enabled.description: Load Kubernetes IngressRoute provider

ingressClass.description: When the parameter is set, only resources containing an annotation with the same value are processed. Otherwise, resources missing the annotation, having an empty value, or the value traefik are processed. It will also set required annotation on Dashboard and Healthcheck IngressRoute when enabled.

namespaces.description: Array of namespaces to watch. If left empty, Traefik watches all namespaces. . When using `rbac.namespaced`, it will watch helm release namespace and namespaces listed in this array.

nativeLBByDefault.description: Defines whether to use Native Kubernetes load-balancing mode by default.

burst.description: Maximum burst of requests to the Kubernetes API server (v3.7.3+). Defaults to 100.

crossProviderNamespaces.description: List of namespaces from which Gateway API routes are allowed to declare TraefikService backendRef references. Requires traefik v3.7.1+.

enabled.description: Enable Traefik Gateway provider for Gateway API

experimentalChannel.description:
Toggles support for the Experimental Channel resources (Gateway API release channels documentation).
This option currently enables support for TCPRoute and TLSRoute.

labelSelector.description: A label selector can be defined to filter on specific GatewayClass objects only.

namespaces.description: Array of namespaces to watch. If left empty, Traefik watches all namespaces. kubernetesGateway provider requires ClusterRole and as a consequence `rbac.namespaced` is not supported.

nativeLBByDefault.description: Defines whether to use Native Kubernetes load-balancing mode by default.

qps.description: Maximum QPS to the Kubernetes API server. A negative value disables client-side ratelimiting (v3.7.3+). Defaults to 50.

hostname.description: This Hostname will get copied to the Gateway status.addresses.

ip.description: This IP will get copied to the Gateway status.addresses, and currently only supports one IP value (IPv4 or IPv6).

enabled.description: The Kubernetes service to copy status addresses from. When using third parties tools like External-DNS, this option can be used to copy the service loadbalancer.status (containing the service's endpoints IPs) to the gateways. Default to Service of this Chart.

allowEmptyServices.description: Allows to return 503 when there are no endpoints available

allowExternalNameServices.description: Allows to reference ExternalName services in Ingress

crossProviderNamespaces.description: List of namespaces from which Ingresses or Services are allowed to declare Middlewares, TLSOptions, or ServersTransport references. Requires traefik v3.7.1+.

disableIngressClassLookup.description: Only for Traefik v3.0, Deprecated since v3.1. See [upstream documentation](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/v3.0/providers/kubernetes-ingress/#disableingressclasslookup)

enabled.description: Load Kubernetes Ingress provider

ingressClass.description: When ingressClass is set, only Ingresses containing an annotation with the same value are processed. Otherwise, Ingresses missing the annotation, having an empty value, or the value traefik are processed.

hostname.description: Hostname used for Kubernetes Ingress endpoints

ip.description: IP used for Kubernetes Ingress endpoints

namespaces.description: Array of namespaces to watch. If left empty, Traefik watches all namespaces. . When using `rbac.namespaced`, it will watch helm release namespace and namespaces listed in this array.

nativeLBByDefault.description: Defines whether to use Native Kubernetes load-balancing mode by default.

publishedService.description: IP used for Kubernetes Ingress endpoints

enabled.description:
Enable [publishedService](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/reference/install-configuration/providers/kubernetes/kubernetes-ingress/#ingressendpointpublishedservice),
usually with the Service provided by this Chart. It's possible to use it with an external Service using pathOverride.

pathOverride.description:
Override path of Kubernetes Service used to copy status from. Format: namespace/servicename.
Default to Service deployed with this Chart.

strictPrefixMatching.description: Defines whether to make prefix matching strictly comply with the Kubernetes Ingress specification.

allowCrossNamespaceResources.description: Allow Ingress to reference resources (e.g. ConfigMaps, Secrets) in different namespaces (default: false)

allowSnippetAnnotations.description: Enables parsing and adding -snippet annotations/directives (default: false)

certAuthFilePath.description: Kubernetes certificate authority file path (not needed for in-cluster client)

clientBodyBufferSize.description: Default buffer size for reading client request body in bytes (default: 16384)

controllerClass.description: Ingress Class Controller value this controller satisfies

customHTTPErrors.description: Defines which HTTP status codes should result in calling the default backend to return an error page

defaultBackendService.description: Service used to serve HTTP requests not matching any known server name (catch-all). Takes the form 'namespace/name'

disableSvcExternalName.description: Disable support for Services of type ExternalName

enabled.description: Enable Kubernetes Ingress NGINX provider

endpoint.description: Kubernetes server endpoint (required for external cluster client)

globalAllowedResponseHeaders.description: List of allowed response headers inside the custom headers annotations

globalAuthUrl.description: URL to the service that provides authentication for all the locations. Per ingress auth-url annotation has precedence over this option.

httpEntryPoint.description: Defines the EntryPoint to use for HTTP requests

httpsEntryPoint.description: Defines the EntryPoint to use for HTTPS requests

ingressClass.description: Name of the ingress class this controller satisfies

ingressClassByName.description: Define if Ingress Controller should watch for Ingress Class by Name together with Controller Class

ipAllowListStrategy.description: When set, the strategy is applied to every generated IPAllowList middleware.

depth.description: Number of trusted proxy hops to skip when extracting the client IP from the X-Forwarded-For header. 0 disables depth-based extraction. (default: 0)

excludedIPS.description: List of IPs to exclude when scanning the X-Forwarded-For header to find the client IP.

ipv6Subnet.description: IPv6 subnet size used to group IPv6 addresses when checking the allow list. 0 disables subnet grouping.

enabled.description: Enable ModSec engine. Requires Traefik Hub >= v3.20.0-ea.8.

proxyBodySize.description: Default maximum size of a client request body in bytes (default: 1048576)

proxyBufferSize.description: Default buffer size for reading the response body in bytes (default: 8192)

proxyBuffering.description: Defines whether to enable response buffering (default: false)

proxyBuffersNumber.description: Default number of buffers for reading a response (default: 4)

proxyConnectTimeout.description: Amount of time to wait until a connection to a server can be established. Unitless, in seconds (default: 60)

proxyNextUpstream.description: Defines in which cases a request should be retried (default: "error timeout")

proxyNextUpstreamTimeout.description: Limits the total elapsed time to retry the request. Unitless, in seconds (default: 0)

proxyNextUpstreamTries.description: Limits the number of possible tries if the backend server does not reply (default: 3)

proxyReadTimeout.description: Amount of time between two successive read operations. Unitless, in seconds (default: 60)

proxyRequestBuffering.description: Defines whether to enable request buffering (default: false)

proxySendTimeout.description: Amount of time between two successive write operations. Unitless, in seconds (default: 60)

enabled.description:
Enable publishService. Service fronting the Ingress controller, used to set the load-balancer status of Ingress objects.
Usually the Service provided by this Chart. It's possible to use it with an external Service using pathOverride.

pathOverride.description:
Override path of Kubernetes Service used to copy status from. Format: namespace/servicename.
Default to Service deployed with this Chart.

publishStatusAddress.description: Customized address (or addresses, separated by comma) to set as the load-balancer status of Ingress objects this controller satisfies

strictValidatePathType.description: Defines whether to reject the entire ingress when any path contains regex characters and pathType is Prefix or Exact (default: true)

token.description: Kubernetes bearer token (not needed for in-cluster client). It accepts either a token value or a file path to the token

upstreamKeepaliveTimeout.description: Defines the idle timeout for keep-alive connections to upstream servers. Unitless, in seconds (default: 60)

watchIngressWithoutClass.description: Define if Ingress Controller should also watch for Ingresses without an IngressClass or the annotation specified

watchNamespace.description: Single namespace the controller watches for updates to Kubernetes objects. Mutually exclusive with watchNamespaceSelector.

watchNamespaceSelector.description: Select namespaces the controller watches for updates to Kubernetes objects. Mutually exclusive with watchNamespace.

precedence.description: Defines the routing precedence between providers. See [upstream documentation](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/reference/install-configuration/providers/overview/#routing-precedence) for the default order.

additionalServices.description:
Can be used to create multiple Service.
See EXAMPLES.md for more details.

annotations.description: Additional annotations applied to both TCP and UDP services (e.g. for cloud provider specific config)

annotationsTCP.description: Additional annotations for TCP service only

annotationsUDP.description: Additional annotations for UDP service only

labels.description: Additional service labels (e.g. for filtering Service by custom labels)

nameOverride.description: Override the default Service name. Useful for adopting an existing Service (e.g., during migration from another ingress controller).

single.description: When set to false, it will create two Service, one for TCP and one for UDP.

spec.description:
Additional entries here will be added to the Service [spec](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.35/#servicespec-v1-core).
Cannot contain selector or ports entries.

serviceAccount.description: The service account the pods will use to interact with the Kubernetes API

serviceAccountAnnotations.description: Additional serviceAccount annotations (e.g. for oidc authentication)

tlsOptions.description:
TLS Options are created as [TLSOption CRDs](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/reference/routing-configuration/kubernetes/crd/tls/tlsoption/)
When using `labelSelector`, you'll need to set labels on tlsOption accordingly.
See EXAMPLE.md for details.

tlsStore.description:
TLS Store are created as [TLSStore CRDs](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/reference/routing-configuration/kubernetes/crd/tls/tlsstore/).
This is useful if you want to set a default certificate. See EXAMPLE.md for details.

tolerations.description: Tolerations allow the scheduler to schedule pods with matching taints.

topologySpreadConstraints.description:
You can use topology spread constraints to control
how Pods are spread across your cluster among failure-domains.
This example topologySpreadConstraints forces the scheduler to put traefik pods
on nodes where no other traefik pods are scheduled.
- labelSelector:
matchLabels:
app.kubernetes.io/name: '{{ template "traefik.name" . }}'
maxSkew: 1
topologyKey: kubernetes.io/hostname
whenUnsatisfiable: DoNotSchedule

versionOverride.description: This field overrides the default version extracted from image.tag. Required when pinning by `image.digest`, since the version cannot be derived from a digest.

volumes.description:
Add volumes to the traefik pod. The volume name will be passed to tpl.
This can be used to mount a cert pair or a configmap that holds a config.toml file.
After the volume has been mounted, add the configs into traefik by using the `additionalArguments` list below, eg:
`additionalArguments:
- "--providers.file.filename=/config/dynamic.toml"
- "--ping"
- "--ping.entrypoint=web"`
- name: public-cert
mountPath: "/certs"
type: secret
- name: '{{ printf "%s-configs" .Release.Name }}'
mountPath: "/config"
type: configMap
